svg.text: Sequential · Concurrent · Handoff · Group Chat · Magentic
Pipeline — each agent builds on the previous output
Agents execute one after another in a defined order. Full conversation
history passes to each subsequent agent for context-aware processing.
BuildSequential() · Full history passed to each agent · Supports HITL via ApprovalRequiredAIFunction · Ideal for data pipelines & multi-stage reasoning
Use cases: Document review · Translation chains · Data processing pipelines · Multi-stage reasoning
Fan-out — agents work in parallel on the same task
Multiple agents process the same input simultaneously and independently.
Results are automatically aggregated. Great for diverse perspectives.
BuildConcurrent() · All agents run simultaneously · Automatic result aggregation · Independent processing — no shared state
Use cases: Brainstorming · Ensemble reasoning · Voting systems · Multi-perspective analysis · Translations
Mesh — agents transfer control based on context
Agents hand off the conversation to each other dynamically. No central
orchestrator — each agent decides when to transfer based on expertise.
CreateHandoffBuilderWith() · WithHandoffs() · HandoffAgentExecutor injects handoff tools · Full context preserved · No central authority
Use cases: Customer support routing · Expert systems · Dynamic delegation · Multi-domain tutoring
⚠ vs Agent-as-Tools: Handoff transfers full ownership. Agent-as-Tools keeps a primary agent in control delegating subtasks.
Star — orchestrator coordinates who speaks next
Agents collaborate in a shared conversation coordinated by a central manager.
Supports round-robin, prompt-based, or custom speaker selection strategies.
CreateGroupChatBuilderWith() · RoundRobinGroupChatManager · Custom ShouldTerminateAsync() · Context broadcast after each turn
Use cases: Content creation · Writer-reviewer loops · Collaborative problem-solving · Multi-perspective QA
Dynamic — manager agent plans and coordinates specialized agents at runtime
A manager agent dynamically creates and coordinates a plan, invoking specialized agents as needed.
Unlike Group Chat, the manager has full planning capability and treats other agents as callable tools.
Specialized Agent Pool (called as tools by the Manager)
Manager creates dynamic execution plan · Specialized agents invoked as tools · Adaptive — plan can change at runtime · Most flexible pattern · Currently Python-only
Use cases: Complex research tasks · Adaptive workflows · Dynamic planning · Tasks requiring multiple specialized skills determined at runtime
All orchestrations support HITL via RequestInfoEvent. Wrap sensitive tools with ApprovalRequiredAIFunction — the workflow pauses, emits an event, and resumes after human approval/rejection.
Sequential → Strict order matters, each step depends on the last
Concurrent → Need diverse perspectives on the same input, no dependencies
Handoff → Dynamic routing between domain specialists, no central authority needed
Group Chat → Iterative refinement with centralized coordination
Magentic → Complex tasks needing adaptive planning at runtime
